activities.title: Pu'ukohola Heiau NHS
activities.notes: Kawaihae, 20 min north. FREE. Largest restored heiau in Hawaii, built 1790–91 by Kamehameha I. STROLLER OK — paved accessible loop from parking to heiau base. HEAT: fully exposed, no shade — go early, 30 min max outside, back in AC by 10am. Closed-toe shoes, hat, sunscreen, cold water. Watch Heiki closely — retreat at first sign of fussiness. Open 8:30am–4:30pm. Visitor center + accessible restrooms on site.
activities.location: Pu'ukohola Heiau National Historic Site, Kawaihae, HI 96743

activities.title: Spencer Beach
activities.notes: Right next door to Heiau — ideal cool-down stop after. Calm reef-protected water — perfect for wading and cooling off overheated toddler. STROLLER OK — flat sand, paved access. Sandals + water shoes for rocky water entry. Lifeguards weekends + holidays 9:30am–4:45pm. Restrooms, showers, playground on site. This IS the cool-down — let Heiki splash, no rush, stay as long as he's happy.

activities.title: Kings' Shops — pick up lava rocks for Wednesday craft
activities.notes: 15 min from home, on the way back from Heiau area. Look for: smooth pāhoehoe pieces (safe for little hands, good painting surface), a chunk of ʻaʻā (rough contrast for sorting), and pumice if available (lightweight, porous, floats in water — great toddler demo). Gift shops or any local rock vendor in the complex.
activities.location: Kings' Shops, 250 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa Village, HI 96738

activities.title: Manta Ray Night Snorkel
activities.notes: BOOKED — confirm pickup time. Grandma on toddler/bedtime duty.
activities.location: Honokohau Small Boat Harbor, Kailua-Kona, HI 96740

activities.title: Manta Magic - Moonlight Manta Magic Tour with Hawaii Oceanic
activities.notes:
Booking #328661566 

 View details: https://fareharbor.com/embeds/book/hawaiioceanic/items/63264/booking/20af9e90-eeaa-4957-a887-5aff11caab5e/

activities.title: Pool time at home
activities.notes: After beach and errands — rest, hydrate, backyard or community pool before manta night.

activities.title: Home reading
activities.notes: Books from home. Quiet morning in before the walk.

activities.title: Waikoloa Dry Forest walk — SHORT
activities.notes: HEAT + TERRAIN ADVISORY: rough uneven lava throughout, no stroller, carrier required. Fully exposed, hot and dry — highest heat-rash risk of the week given no shade and no water nearby. OPTION A: skip it — replace with pool morning (recommended if Heiki is still recovering). OPTION B: go only if cool and early — depart by 8am, 20 min max, abort immediately if Heiki fusses. Complete digital waiver at waikoloadryforest.org before going. NOT a guided tour day (first Fridays only). Closed-toe shoes, lots of water, carrier. Call ahead: (808) 494-2208. Entrance: Quarry Road, Waikoloa.
activities.location: Waikoloa Dry Forest Initiative, Waikoloa, HI 96738

activities.title: Waimea town — Anna Ranch + local lunch
activities.notes: Grandma on toddler duty. 30 min north at 2,700ft — completely different climate zone. Anna Ranch Heritage Center or browse the village. Drive Kohala Mountain Road for the lava-to-pasture transition.
activities.location: Anna Ranch Heritage Center, 65-1480 Kawaihae Rd, Kamuela, HI 96743

activities.title: Pool morning (alt. to Dry Forest)
activities.notes: Recommended if Heiki is still recovering from heat. Cool, relaxed, low-risk. Swap with Dry Forest if needed.

activities.title: Lava rock sorting craft
activities.notes: Use the rocks picked up at Kings' Shops on Monday. Sort ʻaʻā (rough) vs pāhoehoe (smooth) — talk viscosity, eruption style, flow rate. Drop pumice in a bowl of water to show it floats. Even a 2-year-old can sort rough vs smooth.

activities.title: Ho'oko Park
activities.notes: 15 min from home. Free playground, open 6:30am. HEAT: go early — aim to arrive by 8:30am and be back in AC by 9:30am. 30 min max. Bring water for Heiki. If playground equipment feels hot to touch, skip it — metal burns.
activities.location: Ho'oko Street Park, Waikoloa Village, HI 96738

activities.title: Petroglyph-inspired art
activities.notes: Paper and crayons at home. Make your own 'carvings' inspired by Saturday's Reserve visit. Ancient Hawaiian mark-making meets toddler scribbles.

activities.title: KPC Dinner — Kamuela Provision Company
activities.notes: Hilton Waikoloa Village, ~10 min. Romantic patio over Waiulua Bay. Upscale surf 'n' turf, locally sourced. Grandma on toddler duty. BOOK NOW: call Hilton or OpenTable.
activities.location: Kamuela Provision Company, Hilton Waikoloa Village, 69-425 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa Village, HI 96738

activities.title: Pool / backyard cool-down
activities.notes: After park and craft morning. Midday in the pool before nap.

activities.title: Hit the road — Ka'u day trip
activities.notes: Adults only. ~2.5 hrs south. Toddler stays home with grandma.

activities.title: Bay View Farm — guided tour
activities.notes: Pahala. Free guided tours 11am + 1pm Mon–Fri — arrive 15 min early to secure a spot. GPS NOTE: address is 96-2694 Wood Valley Road — described as 'hard to find' with limited signage. Confirm route before leaving. Tour covers orchards, sorting, milling, sun-drying, roasting. Free coffee samples included. On-site café: coffee drinks, espresso, pastries, sandwiches with outdoor orchard views. Gift shop. Terrain: mostly flat paved/gravel + unpaved orchard paths. Stroller partial — main area OK. Comfortable walking shoes. Flip-flops fine for tasting room; closed-toe better for orchard paths. Ka'u coffee is award-winning and less touristy than Kona — a genuine find. ~1hr total.

activities.title: South Point / Ka Lae
activities.notes: Southernmost point in the US — 12-mile narrow paved road off Hwy 11 (mile markers 69–70). Pull over for oncoming traffic. NO STROLLER — rocky, uneven lava cliff terrain with no maintained trails or guardrails. Closed-toe sturdy shoes required. BRING: windproof layer (consistently strong winds), water + snacks (NO services at point), sunscreen, fully charged phone with offline maps (cell service unreliable). Secure all loose items — wind will carry things. Leave no valuables in car (break-ins reported). DANGER: Halaea Current is extremely powerful — swimming NOT recommended under any circumstances. Cliff jumping: no guardrails, multiple drownings recorded, no rescue services nearby. Kalalea Heiau on site — sacred, do not touch or sit on walls. Adults-only stop — not suitable with Heiki. Green Sand Beach is an additional 5.5mi hike or paid 4WD shuttle (~$15–25/person cash) — add 2+ hrs if going.
activities.location: Ka Lae (South Point), South Point Rd, Na'alehu, HI 96772

activities.title: Punalu'u Black Sand Beach
activities.notes: Black basalt sand + olivine flecks — formed by lava entering ocean. Paved parking, flat easy access. STROLLER OK — paved lot and flat beach access. FOOTWEAR: sandals or water shoes strongly recommended — black sand gets EXTREMELY HOT in sun (burns bare feet fast). Lava rocks sharp at water's edge. TURTLES: sea turtles haul out daily ~11am–6pm. Federal law: stay at LEAST 10 feet away. Do NOT touch, feed, or approach. Fine up to $10,500. Swimming: currents can be strong, rocky entry difficult. Northeast end of beach has fewest rocks; small boat ramp on left is easiest entry. Check conditions first. High surf + rip currents possible in winter. Portuguese man-o-war ~10 days after full moon. DO NOT take black sand — removing natural material from Hawaii state beaches is illegal. Facilities: restrooms, showers, drinking water, covered pavilions, snack bar. Lifeguards daily 8:30am–5pm. Best for turtles: afternoon. Best for crowds: morning. Off Hwy 11 between mile markers 56–57.
activities.location: Punalu'u Black Sand Beach, Na'alehu, HI 96772

activities.title: Bay View Farm — arrive + self-guided tasting
activities.notes:
Pahala. Open 9–4. Browse the grounds, taste before the tour. Ka'u district: volcanic soil from Kilauea's rain shadow, high elevation, cloud cover. One of the highest-rated coffees in Hawaii.

👫 🌋

activities.title: Puako Petroglyph Field
activities.notes: Holoholokai Beach Park, Mauna Lani — 30 min north. DEPART BY 7am TO BEAT THE HEAT — aim to be done and back in AC by 9am. HEAT: 30 min max on exposed lava. Watch Heiki — leave immediately at first mood drop. Stroller OK for first 0.1mi paved section to replica carvings — that may be enough with heat concerns. Full trail: rough lava, kiawe thorns (pierce thin soles), NO stroller — carrier required. Closed-toe sturdy shoes required. Bring LOTS of water, sunscreen, hat. DO NOT touch authentic petroglyphs — replicas near trailhead are touch-permitted. Parking day use fee may apply. Facilities: restrooms, showers at beach park. Hours: 6:30am–6:30pm. Directions: Queen Kaʻahumanu Hwy → Mauna Lani Dr → North Kaniku Dr → Holoholokai Beach Park.
activities.location: Holoholokai Beach Park, 68-1400 Mauna Lani Dr, Kohala Coast, HI 96743

activities.title: Beach cool-down — Holoholokai or A-Bay
activities.notes: After petroglyphs — stay at Holoholokai Beach Park (already there) or drive back to A-Bay. Let Heiki cool down in the water. Calm beach, shaded spots. Pack a cold drink and snack for the car ride.

activities.title: Pool at home — recovery
activities.notes: Back from petroglyphs early. Pool time before nap to fully cool down.

activities.title: Slow morning + shave ice
activities.notes: Last full day — take it easy. Alii Drive shave ice if time allows. Go early (before 10am) if venturing out — last thing you want is heat rash on departure day. Otherwise: stay home, pool, pack slowly, enjoy the lanai.

activities.title: Pack + souvenir lava rocks
activities.notes: From a local shop, Punalu'u vendors, or HVNP gift shop — NOT from national or state parks.

activities.title: Pool + pack
activities.notes: Last pool session before departure tomorrow. Easy morning, stay cool.

activities.title: Depart ✈️
activities.notes: Safe travels.
activities.location: Ellison Onizuka Kona International Airport, 73-200 Makala Blvd, Kailua-Kona, HI 96740

activities.title: OOO
A-Bay afternoon
activities.notes:
Anchialine pond walk, King's Trail, turtles.

🧒 🌋 🏛️
activities.location: Anaeho'omalu Bay, 69-275 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa, HI 96738
